concerns.text: The plan cites 'JavaScript (Test)' from 'hchautran/javascript' and 'JavaScript (Train)' from 'angie-chen55/javascript-github-code' as primary sources for stratified sampling. These are generic GitHub code dumps without embedded unit tests. The spec (FR-003) and research goal require snippets with associated unit tests to serve as the dynamic oracle. Using these datasets will result in >99% of samples being marked 'untestable_dynamic', failing the requirement to compare static vs. dynamic analysis on the same snippets. The plan must cite a dataset that explicitly pairs code with test cases (e.g., HumanEval-X for other languages, or a verified CodeXGLUE subset with tests).

The plan lists 'BigCode' as 'NO verified source found' and states it will 'use local samples if available'. The Spec (FR-001) and Constitution (Principle VII) mandate that the dataset MUST consist of samples from HumanEval, CodeXGLUE, and BigCode. Relying on 'local samples' or admitting a missing source for a mandated dataset violates the data source requirements. The plan must identify a specific, accessible BigCode dataset URL (e.g., from HuggingFace) that contains code + tests, or explicitly revise the Spec to remove the BigCode mandate if no such verified source exists.

The plan states 'Java samples may be limited' and relies on 'HumanEval and JavaScript datasets' to meet the ≥500 snippet requirement across Python, JS, and Java. HumanEval is Python-only. The cited JavaScript datasets do not contain Java. There is no verified Java dataset with unit tests cited in the plan. This makes the requirement to cover ≥3 languages (SC-003) and the stratified sampling strategy (Constitution Principle VII) impossible to execute as written. A verified Java dataset with unit tests (e.g., from CodeXGLUE or a specific Java HumanEval extension) must be identified.
